extracted_information: The provided content details the optional enablement and configuration of cgroup v2 for Rootless Containers, primarily for resource limiting (CPU, memory, I/O, PIDs). It distinguishes cgroup v2 from process-granularity tools like ulimit and cpulimit by emphasizing container-granularity resource control. Information includes methods to check cgroup v2 status, default distribution support, and steps for enabling cgroup v2 on the host and delegating additional controllers (CPU, CPUSET, I/O) to non-root users.

features.name: Resource Limiting
features.description: Limits consumption of CPU, memory, I/O, and PIDs resources for containers. Example: `docker run --memory 32m`.

features.name: Container-granularity control
features.description: Provides resource limiting at the container level, unlike process-granularity tools (ulimit, cpulimit).

features.name: Controller Delegation
features.description: Allows non-root users to manage specific cgroup controllers like CPU, CPUSET, I/O, memory, and PIDs.
